aliases: Clear Soup Brisket Rice Noodle Set
Sour Spicy Beef Rice Noodle Set

english: Clear Soup Brisket Rice Noodle Set
Sour Spicy Beef Rice Noodle Set

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for rice noodle set meals; FOODON_03315595 (noodle dish) used as the closest broader class. Mixian (rice vermicelli noodle) set with beef toppings and side dishes is a composite dish not individually coded in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW (Noodle-based dishes) is the closest defensible category. No specific code exists for Chinese rice noodle (mixian) set meals in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept maps specifically to a Chinese rice noodle set meal. SNOMED CT coverage for composite Asian set meals is absent as of 2024.

main_ingredients: glutinous rice cake (crispy lava rice cake)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard deluxe set meal comprising approximately 300 g rice noodles with broth and beef topping, 80 g crispy lava rice cake, 80 g pickled radish, and approximately 90 ml drink, totalling roughly 550 g.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo — Rice Noodle Soup with Beef (composite dish reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at confidence 0.95 with review_status 'reviewed'. All per_100g values are taken directly from the HPB-sourced input. The composite set meal (noodles + side dishes + drink) is represented as a single per-100g nutritional profile as provided by HPB Foodo.

gi_source.title: Rice noodles, boiled — GI value 61 (analogous estimate based on rice vermicelli / rice noodle entries)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for a composite Chinese rice noodle set meal. GI of 61 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled rice noodles / rice vermicelli (GI range 58–65, representative value 61). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (61) × available carbohydrate per serving (carbohydrate_g 25.19 per 100g × 550g serving / 100) / 100 = 61 × 138.5g / 100 = 84.5, rounded to 84.8. This is a set-meal composite; actual GL will vary with drink and side-dish carbohydrate content.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 231 mg/100 g; full set meal (~550 g) delivers approximately 1,270 mg sodium, approaching 55% of the 2,300 mg daily limit in a single meal.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced broth volume and avoid additional sauces; the favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio (327 mg K : 231 mg Na per 100 g) partially offsets vascular risk, but total meal sodium remains a concern at full serving size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 25.19 g/100 g with GI ~61 (medium); a full 550 g set meal delivers approximately 138 g available carbohydrate, which is high for a single meal.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 250–300 g of the noodle component, choose clear-soup over braised-beef broth to reduce added sugars and fat, and pair with the pickled radish side to leverage its low glycaemic contribution and fibre content.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Red meat (beef) toppings contribute saturated fat and haem iron; sodium load at full serving is elevated; cholesterol is reported as 0 mg/100 g per HPB data but saturated fat breakdown is unavailable.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should choose the clear-soup brisket or request leaner protein options, limit broth consumption to reduce sodium intake, and be aware that saturated fat data for this composite dish is not available from the HPB source — caution is warranted until full lipid profiling is confirmed.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat (beef) toppings are high in arachidonic acid, which can amplify RA inflammatory pathways; broth sodium may worsen tissue swelling.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute beef toppings with chicken, seafood, or tofu where available, request reduced broth, and verify whether chili or tomato-based condiments are included as nightshades may trigger RA flares.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Deluxe Rice Noodle Set (超满足升级米线套餐) is a Chinese-Singaporean set meal centred on mixian — smooth, round rice noodles made from rice flour — served in a flavourful broth with a choice of three beef preparations: red-braised beef (红烧牛肉), clear-soup beef brisket (清汤牛腩), or sour-and-spicy sliced beef with pickled mustard greens (酸菜肥牛). The set is completed with a crispy lava glutinous rice cake (红糖糍粑), a refreshing pickled radish side (爽口脆萝卜), and a choice of drink. Mixian noodles have a silky, slightly chewy texture and absorb broth flavours readily. The dish is a staple of Yunnan-style Chinese cuisine as adapted for the Singapore hawker and casual-dining context. Nutritionally, the set provides 164 kcal per 100 g with 6.5 g protein, 4.21 g fat, and 25.19 g carbohydrate. Dietary fibre is notable at 3.7 g/100 g, which moderates the glycaemic impact of the rice noodles (GI ~61, medium category). Sodium at 231 mg/100 g is moderate per 100 g but accumulates significantly at full set-meal serving size (~550 g, ~1,270 mg sodium). Potassium at 327 mg/100 g and iron at 2.55 mg/100 g are clinically meaningful. The dish is suitable for most healthy adults as part of a balanced diet; portion control and broth management are recommended for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease.
common_user_queries: What is in the Deluxe Rice Noodle Set?

confidence_reason: All per_100g nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95 with review_status 'reviewed' and provenance 'canonical'. The input record was manually enriched and carries the highest-priority regional source for Singapore dishes. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using the closest analogous food (boiled rice noodles, GI 61) and is clearly documented as an analogous estimate.
notes: 1. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite rice noodle set meal is not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry as of 2026-04-16. 2. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0 mg/100 g per HPB source; this is accepted as confirmed zero per the HPB Foodo database for this dish composite — note that beef toppings would normally contribute some cholesterol, and this value may reflect the averaged composite including broth and side dishes. 3. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo source for this composite dish as of 2026-04-16. 4. GI of 61 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (boiled rice noodles / rice vermicelli); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific composite set meal. 5. Halal status is not confirmed; the dish contains beef and the halal certification of the specific vendor is not verified in the HPB source. 6. The set meal includes a drink (unspecified, three choices) which may add significant sugar and calories not captured in the per_100g composite. 7. Serving size of 550 g is an estimate for a full set meal; actual weight will vary by vendor and portion.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full RESULT JSON from input. Added missing local_names (Malay, Tamil, Hindi, Chinese traditional). Expanded aliases and aliases_by_language. Added GI from University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate, boiled rice noodles GI 61). Added health_context for all three required conditions plus RA. Added ontology with FoodOn and FoodEx2 broader-class codes and gap notes. Documented all -1 fields and cholesterol_mg 0.0 in notes.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match_type values to 'closest_parent'. Verified aliases_by_language strings against flat aliases array.

Iteration 3: Fixed q8 failure — validator reported aliases_by_language entries missing from flat aliases. Root cause: '超滿足升級米线套餐' (mixed simplified/traditional form) was in aliases_by_language chinese array in previous draft but not in flat aliases. Performed exhaustive self-check: collected every string from every language key in aliases_by_language, verified each against flat aliases array, added all missing entries. Flat aliases array now contains all 17 strings present across all aliases_by_language language keys. Self-check confirmed complete.
